mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals persistent household income expansion, which drives consumption and corporate earnings expectations, lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive during months when PAYEMS increases over a 3-year rolling window, or the mean return in those months is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate above neutral levels directly increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and raises debt-service costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive or zero in more than 50 percent of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5 percent over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and a declining unemployment rate indicate robust demand-side momentum concurrent with tightening labor markets, amplifying corporate margin expansion and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and falling UNRATE does not produce positive 20-day forward returns in at least 55 percent of instances over a 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 returns during periods when the economy is operating above trend capacity, because supply-constrained expansions translate operating leverage directly into pricing power and profit growth.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of weeks when INDPRO is accelerating during above-trend expansion periods over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below one million units signals weakening residential construction activity and downstream demand for durables and materials, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are not negative in more than 50 percent of instances when HOUST is below 1000 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices concurrent with stagnant industrial production create a stagflationary margin squeeze where input costs increase without offsetting revenue growth, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and flat or falling INDPRO does not produce negative 20-day forward returns in at least 55 percent of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Trend acceleration in industrial production reflects improving manufacturing utilization and corporate operating leverage, which translates directly into earnings surprises and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns do not occur in more than 55 percent of months when INDPRO is trending upward over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes household consumption contraction and corporate earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when the unemployment trigger fires over a 2-year rolling window.
